title: Zero is not absence
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: The stock is zero — that is a known number, not missing information. And the code claims it was never counted.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
hint2.changed-data: The zero stays. It is the reason this lesson exists.
why.no-explicit: The checks are still not explicit.
hint2.no-explicit: A condition with just a name asks whether it is truthy. It should ask something specific.
why.wrong-stock: The stock is reported as missing.
hint2.wrong-stock: Zero cups is a known fact. It is not the same as we never counted.
hint3.wrong-stock: A missing value means null or undefined. Ask for those exactly.
why.wrong-note: The empty note is not spotted.
hint2.wrong-note: The note is empty text. Compare it with empty text explicitly.
hint3.wrong-note: Here the shorthand check happens to give the right answer. But with a note of three spaces it would still claim there is a note.
why.wrong-price: The discount is reported as not set.
hint2.wrong-price: A zero percent discount is a decision. No discount is the absence of a decision. The two are not the same.
why.wrong-count: There are not three lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Three checks. Stock of zero cups, an empty note, a discount of zero percent.
blocks.1.text: The code claims the stock was never counted and that no discount was set. Both are untrue — we counted, and we decided. The answer just happens to be zero.
blocks.2.text: A condition does not need true
blocks.3.text: Anything can sit inside the brackets, not just a comparison. Whatever you put there, the language reduces to truthy or falsy before deciding.
blocks.4.text: That is why if (stock) runs. The question is what it actually asks — and it asks something quite different from what it looks like.
blocks.6.text: Only six values count as falsy. Everything else, absolutely everything, is truthy.
blocks.8.text: An empty array is truthy. An empty object is truthy. The text "false" is truthy, because it is five characters of text. A negative number too, because it is not zero.
blocks.9.text: This is where the bug comes from
blocks.10.text: if (stock) looks like is there stock. But it asks whether the value is truthy — and zero is not.
blocks.11.text: So the code works while the stock is fifteen. The moment it hits zero it starts claiming it was never counted. Exactly when the answer matters most.
blocks.12.quote: Zero is a known answer. Absence is the lack of an answer. The shorthand check cannot tell them apart.
blocks.14.text: Make the three checks explicit. Missing means null or undefined. An empty note means empty text.
blocks.15.text: After that, zero cups should read In stock: 0, and a zero discount should read Discount: 0.
